steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence opens with a participial phrase: "Woven from recycled yarn and hand tufted..." Whatever comes in the blank must be the grammatical subject that phrase modifies, plus a linking verb to connect to the adjective phrase "so lush and tactilely inviting..." that follows.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• "the topological tapestries of Argentine textile artist Alexandra Kehayoglou are": The woven, tufted things are the tapestries — making them the subject resolves the modifier correctly, and "are" provides the required linking verb. ✓
  • "the Argentine textile artist Alexandra Kehayoglou creates topological tapestries that are": The artist was not woven from recycled yarn — this creates a dangling modifier. ✗
  • "when she creates her topological tapestries. Argentine textile artist Alexandra Kehayoglou makes them": No verb follows before the adjective phrase, leaving the sentence a fragment. ✗
  • "Alexandra Kehayoglou is an Argentine textile artist whose topological tapestries are": Again places the artist as the implied actor of the participial phrase — another dangling modifier. ✗
That matches the correct answer — A is correct.
